title: Adaptive learning and modality change
preconditions: A household and learner profile exist.
The learner has a fraction-equivalence KC target and low recent mastery.
Rollout policy allows non-text modalities, or the expected fallback is documented.
setup_path: Run the seeded household setup through scripts/rehearse_proof_scenarios.py.
Use Avery from the proof fixture; the script records weak observations against KC-FRAC-EQUIV.
Keep the default rollout policy unless intentionally rehearsing text-only fallback.
execution_path: POST /api/content/generate for a baseline practice item.
POST /api/learners/{student_id}/observations twice with weak evidence tied to the generation_id.
POST /api/observability/adaptation/modality-routing/inspect with the same learner and a visual worked-example request.
POST /api/content/generate for the follow-up visual worked example.
visible_outcome: The follow-up response uses a different modality, normally diagram, or the inspection explicitly shows a rollout text fallback.
approval_hooks: Non-text modality rollout policy can constrain the effective modality to text_only_fallback.

rehearsal_steps.action: Request a worked example for equivalent fractions with default routing.
rehearsal_steps.expected_observation: Initial content is text or narrative with a visible modality directive in the response metadata.
rehearsal_steps.proof_signal: The backend chooses the delivery path and exposes presentation-ready labels.

rehearsal_steps.action: Submit weak observations: low completion, hints used, repeated error, high load, and low confidence.
rehearsal_steps.expected_observation: Observation evidence updates learner state and records overload or stalled-progress signals.
rehearsal_steps.proof_signal: Outcome evidence, not frontend logic, drives the next routing decision.

rehearsal_steps.action: Inspect modality routing for the same KC and move.
rehearsal_steps.expected_observation: Routing explanation shows diagram or narrative as the selected/effective modality, or a rollout fallback to text.
rehearsal_steps.proof_signal: The modality change is legible and attributable to learner-private priors that remain local.

rehearsal_steps.action: Generate the follow-up attempt and submit improved observations.
rehearsal_steps.expected_observation: The follow-up uses a materially different representation; improved evidence changes the adaptation summary.
rehearsal_steps.proof_signal: The scenario demonstrates a visible before/after teaching change.

expected_observations: Text, narrative, and diagram produce visibly different learner experiences.
Modality decisions can be inspected from the operator surface.
Rollout fallback to text is explicit when non-text modalities are disabled.
success_criteria: The second attempt changes modality or records a policy-constrained fallback.
The explanation names the routing reason and rollout effect.
No learner-private evidence is present in content-library artifacts.
reset_notes: Use a fresh learner profile or clear modality-routing priors to repeat the before/after shape.
